opp.ais_why:
• HP Hood opportunity is anchored on standard Sourcing, SRM, and Spend modules via a structured RFI/RFP process with no explicit mention of AI, Merlin, ANA, or agentic capabilities.
• Next steps focus on NDA execution and RFI response covering traditional procurement scope rather than AI differentiation.
• Early Qualified stage with formal RFP not expected until Q3, suggesting a process-driven evaluation rather than AI-led urgency.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case documented in Salesforce or available call content. The opportunity Description field is empty, and no custom fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) were readable in this org. The Next_Step__c mentions HP Hood will conduct internal evaluation and business case development in Q3/Q4, implying metrics discovery is still ahead. Without transcripts from the two May touchpoints, we cannot confirm whether pain or savings were articulated. The highest-leverage immediate move is to run a discovery session that extracts the procurement spend baseline, current vendor costs, cycle time, maverick spend rate, and supplier risk exposure, then quantifies the Zycus ROI in writing before the RFI is released.

sources: SF task reference to Dennis Forest (25 Jun 2026)
champion.narrative: No champion identified in Contact Roles (0 buyer-side roles on this opp), synthesis-map fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c unreadable), or available call transcripts. The recent task log (25 Jun 2026) references Dennis Forest (Manager, Procurement per account contact list), suggesting he may be the primary thread, but we have no evidence of advocacy, EB/DM access, or active engagement beyond the task reference. The two May touchpoints (6 May, 20 May, no transcripts) likely involved Dennis Forest or another sourcing contact, but without recordings we cannot assess advocacy, internal influence, or whether they are pushing Zycus versus neutral evaluation. A champion must be developed immediately: during the NDA and RFI clarification, identify who internally is driving the vendor evaluation, who has access to the EB (likely the Head of IT or Supply Chain leadership), and who will advocate for Zycus through the shortlist and RFP. The move is to ask the current contact (Dennis Forest or the sourcing lead) to co-author the RFI response and to introduce us to the evaluation committee chair, converting passive engagement into active partnership.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (vendor comparison and shortlist planned)
competition.narrative: No competitor documented in Salesforce (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, and free-text competition fields unreadable or empty) or available call transcripts (2 touchpoints in May, 0 transcripts). The Next_Step__c states that HP Hood's internal sourcing leadership will compare vendors and shortlist for deeper engagement, and a formal RFP is planned in Q3, implying a multi-vendor evaluation is underway or imminent, but no rival is named. Without transcripts from the May touchpoints, we cannot confirm whether HP Hood mentioned incumbent systems, peer platforms (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer), or a do-nothing/status-quo alternative. The RFI and shortlist phases (next 30-60 days) are when the competitive field will crystallize. The immediate move is to ask during the RFI clarification call which other vendors are being evaluated, what their current procurement stack looks like (incumbent to be replaced or greenfield), and how they are addressing their problem today. This intel must be captured before the RFI response is submitted so we can position differentiation (Merlin AI, Source-to-Pay breadth, onboarding speed) against known rivals.

sources: SF Products__c (pain inferred from module scope)
identify_pain.narrative: No pain documented in Salesforce (Description empty, Customer_Business_Problem__c and related fields unreadable) or available call transcripts (2 touchpoints in May, 0 transcripts). The Products__c scope (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk) implies pain around sourcing fragmentation, SRM visibility, spend control, procurement inefficiency, requisition approval delays, and supplier risk, but none of this is confirmed as buyer-stated pain. The Next_Step__c indicates HP Hood is early in vendor evaluation (RFI not yet shared), meaning pain discovery is still ahead or happened in the unrecorded May touchpoints. The account-level contact list includes sourcing, procurement, supply chain, and IT leaders, suggesting cross-functional pain (operational in sourcing/procurement, financial in spend leakage, technical in integration/onboarding), but no confirmed pain owner. The immediate move is to run a structured discovery call with sourcing leadership (likely the contact who attended the May touchpoints) to extract the top 3 pain points driving this evaluation, quantify them (current cost, cycle time, error rate), and name the executive sponsor of each pain area. This discovery must happen before the RFI response is submitted, so our answers directly address their articulated pain.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (NDA, business case, Q4 budget alignment)
paper_process.narrative: The Next_Step__c states that NDA finalization is underway (legal review of MNDA internal to Zycus, confirm signatory details, route via DocuSign, execute so RFI can be released), indicating that contracting mechanics have begun. The process includes business case development and Q4 budget alignment, implying a formal approval and likely an MSA/SOW cycle post-RFP. However, no InfoSec review, ERP/systems integration deep-dive, procurement channel (direct vs. through a partner/SI), or Legal redline expectations are documented. The Next_Step__c mentions IT requirements in the RFI (onboarding, support model, IT fit), but no InfoSec questionnaire or integration scoping is explicitly called out. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not readable in this org, so we do not know whether HP Hood has an incumbent contract expiring that would force the timeline. The move is to ask during the RFI clarification whether InfoSec and IT integration reviews run in parallel with the RFP or sequentially afterward, and to confirm the procurement path (direct buy vs. through a sourcing partner) so we can align resources and timeline.

sources: Account contact list (candidate titles, no confirmed opp involvement)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified in Contact Roles (0 buyer-side roles on this opp) or synthesis-map fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c not readable). The account-level contact list includes senior titles (Frank Wiggins, Group VP IT / Head of IT; Vardhaman Bawari, Sr. Director Supply Chain; Joe Ferraro, Supply Chain Director) who may hold budget authority, but none are confirmed as involved in THIS opportunity's scope. The Next_Step__c indicates budget alignment is planned for Q4 2026, meaning the EB conversation has not yet occurred. Without call transcripts, we cannot name who participated in the May touchpoints or who controls the $250k decision. The immediate move is to ask the current contact (likely sourcing leadership) who will approve the budget and secure an introduction before the RFI is released, so pricing and commercials are anchored to the right authority.

sources: Avoma manifest (2 touchpoints, no transcripts to reveal committee)
decision_process.narrative: The Next_Step__c documents a clear buyer-driven process: (1) NDA execution to unlock RFI release, (2) RFI questionnaire from HP Hood with structured vendor responses, (3) internal evaluation by core sourcing leadership to compare vendors and shortlist, (4) discovery deep-dive and tailored demo for shortlisted vendors, (5) formal RFP in Q3 2026 if shortlisted, with business case development and Q4 budget alignment. This is a standard enterprise procurement motion (RFI → shortlist → RFP → budget → contract). However, no approval chain, signatory roles, or Legal/InfoSec/IT gate sequence is documented. We do not know whether sourcing leadership alone can shortlist or whether Finance/IT/C-level must weigh in, nor do we know the committee structure. The two May touchpoints (6 May, 20 May, no transcripts) likely involved sourcing stakeholders, but without recordings we cannot map who attended or their roles. The move is to ask during the RFI clarification call who sits on the evaluation committee, who owns the final vendor selection, and what approvals are required to move from shortlist to contract.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (5-phase evaluation plan, updated 5 Jun 2026)
decision_criteria.narrative: The Next_Step__c outlines a structured evaluation: HP Hood will share an RFI questionnaire covering Sourcing, SRM, Spend, IT, onboarding, and support model; internal sourcing leadership will compare vendors and shortlist; deeper engagement will follow with process walkthrough and tailored demo; and a formal RFP is expected in Q3 if Zycus is shortlisted. This sequence implies criteria around sourcing functionality, SRM capabilities, spend visibility, IT integration, onboarding ease, and support quality. However, no confirmed BRD, scoring rubric, or buyer-stated priority ordering exists yet. The Products__c field lists iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk, suggesting broad platform scope, but we do not know which modules are must-haves versus nice-to-haves or how AI/Merlin fits the evaluation. The RFI response (imminent, per Next_Step__c) is the moment to map and validate criteria; the move is to request the RFI questionnaire as soon as the NDA executes and to pre-align our response on their top 3 decision factors before submitting.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS fields populated (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null), and no call transcripts available to assess buyer AI appetite. The Next_Step__c does not mention AI, automation, or innovation as evaluation criteria. Products__c scope is broad (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk) but does not explicitly call out Merlin. Without evidence, AI fit is unconfirmed. The immediate move is to assess AI appetite during the RFI clarification call: ask whether their evaluation includes AI/ML capabilities, automation of negotiation or contract workflows, or innovation roadmap fit. If yes, position Merlin as a Phase 2 centerpiece; if no, introduce it as a value-add and gauge interest.

The deal is early and on pace: NDA execution and RFI preparation are the current focus, consistent with Formal Evaluation stage and 188 days to close.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live (last activity 25 Jun, 1 day ago) and on pace (Formal Evaluation with 188 days to close, NDA and RFI underway per Next_Step__c). Two May touchpoints (6 May, 20 May) indicate early buyer engagement, but no transcripts available to assess depth or sentiment. Next_Step__c (updated 5 Jun 2026) documents a clear 5-phase plan aligned with enterprise motion, signaling buyer commitment to a structured evaluation. However, MEDDPICC coverage is thin (no EB, champion, pain, competitive intel), and the deal is single-threaded into one procurement contact. Confidence is lowered by lack of call content and multi-threading, but the process structure and recent activity keep the deal on track.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 8 May 2026 (49 days ago), currently in Formal Evaluation stage, 188 days to close date 31 Dec 2026. Last activity 25 Jun (1 day ago). Two touchpoints documented (6 May and 20 May, both completed without recording). Next_Step__c updated as recently as 5 Jun 2026, outlining a clear 5-phase path: NDA finalization, RFI response, internal buyer evaluation, discovery deep-dive, and Q3 RFP with Q4 budget alignment. The timeline is consistent with a 12-15 month enterprise motion; the deal is appropriately early (RFI has not yet been shared, NDA still in legal review), and the close date allows ample runway for the full motion.

north_star_verdict.headline: EARLY-stage Formal Evaluation remains on plan: NDA in legal, RFI prep underway, 188-day runway fits 5-phase enterprise motion. Buyer engaged twice in May; multi-vendor RFP expected Q3. No champion/EB mapped yet—appropriate for current phase.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage Formal Evaluation with strong process structure (Next_Step__c) but critical discovery gaps: no EB, champion, quantified pain, or competitive intel. Two May touchpoints yielded no transcripts, leaving MEDDPICC largely unconfirmed. The immediate levers to raise win probability are: (1) multi-thread beyond the single procurement contact (Dennis Forest) by securing intro to sourcing leadership, IT (Frank Wiggins), and supply chain (Vardhaman Bawari or Joe Ferraro) during the RFI clarification call; (2) extract competitive shortlist and incumbent stack before submitting the RFI response, so we can position Merlin AI and Source-to-Pay breadth as differentiation; (3) run a structured discovery session that quantifies pain (current spend leakage, cycle time, supplier risk exposure) and maps the EB and approval chain before the internal evaluation phase begins.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified in Salesforce fields or available call transcripts (2 May touchpoints, 0 transcripts). The Next_Step__c indicates HP Hood will compare vendors and shortlist during internal evaluation (phase 3, following RFI response), and a formal RFP is planned in Q3 2026 if Zycus is shortlisted, implying a multi-vendor process is underway or imminent. Without competitive intel, we cannot assess threat level or positioning. The immediate move is to extract the competitive shortlist and the incumbent/current-state stack during the RFI clarification call, before responses are submitted, so we can position Zycus differentiation (Merlin AI negotiation agent, Source-to-Pay breadth, onboarding speed, Spend+SRM+Sourcing integration) against known rivals. Until then, the competitive field is unknown.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: No AI excitement signal documented (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null). The Products__c scope includes iRisk and the full Source-to-Pay suite but does not explicitly call out Merlin AI or agentic capabilities. Without call transcripts, we cannot assess whether HP Hood articulated AI appetite, automation pain, or interest in negotiation intelligence during the May touchpoints. The RFI (imminent) is the moment to surface Merlin: if the RFI includes questions on AI, automation, or innovation, lead with the Merlin negotiation agent (supplier price history, contract intelligence, autonomous negotiation workflows) as a differentiator. If the RFI is silent on AI, proactively include a Merlin value-add section in the response and propose a Merlin-specific demo during the discovery deep-dive phase.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: HP Hood's evaluation criteria (per Next_Step__c) center on Sourcing, SRM, Spend visibility, IT integration, onboarding, and support model. The Products__c scope (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, iRisk) aligns well with their stated RFI coverage areas, suggesting strong platform fit. However, no confirmed BRD, buyer-stated priorities, or success criteria exist yet. The RFI response (imminent) is the moment to validate fit and surface any gaps. Without call transcripts, we cannot assess whether AI/Merlin appetite exists or whether they have unique requirements (e.g., dairy/food industry compliance, ERP integration with specific systems, supplier diversity mandates) that could expose us.
